title: Radiographic reporting in adolescent idiopathic scoliosis: Is there a discrepancy between radiologists' and spine surgeons' Cobb angle measurements?
venue: Canadian Journal of Surgery (Canadian Spine Society abstract)
abstract: Cobb angle measurement is a standard method for quantification of scoliosis in adolescent idiopathic scoliosis (AIS). This study investigated the agreement in Cobb angle measurement between radiologist and the treating spine surgeon. A retrospective audit of radiographs for 80 patients with AIS was performed; radiographic reports were compared. Agreement of Cobb angle between spine surgeons was excellent (ICC 0.96). Agreement between spine surgeons and radiologists was poor (ICC 0.65).
out_of_scope_note: Clinical-radiology inter-rater reliability study comparing radiologist vs spine-surgeon Cobb-angle measurements in adolescent idiopathic scoliosis — orthopedic clinical scope, not cognitive/perceptual/computational neuroscience.
